title: Dephosphorylation of 5´-ends of DNA using Antarctic Phosphatase (NEB #M0289)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8o9vb","text":"This is the protocol for dephosphorylation of 5'-ends of DNA using AnP (Antarctic Phosphatase - M0289).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8slr4","text":"Dephosphorylation of 5' -ends of DNA in Restriction Enzyme Reaction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":67}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aqhde","text":"The phosphate can be added directly into the digestion reaction during or after DNA digestion","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dp4t6","text":"Antarctic Phosphatase is active in all NEB restriction enzyme buffers only when supplemented with Antarctic Phosphatase Reaction Buffer, which provides Zn2+ required for enzyme activity","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9av82","text":"The restriction enzyme should be heat inactivated at the same time as the phosphatase after digest and dephosphorylation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2ce9j","text":"If restriction enzyme cannot be heat inactivated, DNA purification is required before ligation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"90d5h","text":"Please refer to the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for health and environmental hazards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare a 20 µL reaction as follows: A B 1 COMPONENT AMOUNT 2 DNA 1 pmol of DNA ends* 3 Antarctic Phosphatase Reaction Buffer (10X) 2 μl 4 Antarctic Phosphatase 5 units 5 H2O, purified to 20 μl** * Note: 1 pmol of DNA ends is about 1 μg of a 3 kb plasmid. ** Scale larger reaction volumes proportionally.
Stop reaction by heat-inactivation at 80 °C for 00:02:00 .
creator: New England   Biolabs
